annotations.text: Several factors are likely to have contributed: difficulties with adherence, an interaction between rifampin and ritonavir, and problems with dosing.
Mutations associated with resistance to protease inhibitors and NRTIs, such as lamivudine (M184V) and thymidine analogues, are likely to have developed in this patient.
When it is determined that antiretroviral therapy has failed in a child, a full assessment of the possible reasons for failure should be conducted.
A change in the drug regimen should be guided by the patient's drug history and genotyping (when available).
In this patient, multiple protease-inhibitor mutations were detected. Since the child had not previously received an NNRTI, a simple switch to two NRTIs and an NNRTI might be appropriate.
This case illustrates the problems that can occur when HIV infection in pediatric patients is not diagnosed and treated properly.
Alternatively, a regimen that includes an NNRTI plus a newer protease inhibitor might also be effective.

Darunavir is a nonpeptide protease inhibitor that binds rapidly to protease at a unique site and dissociates slowly, resulting in a binding strength that is two times as high as that of other protease inhibitors.
The mutations seen in this patient's virus confer intermediate resistance to darunavir and tipranavir.

Especially with young children, one may not need to use the term HIV but could instead tell them that they have an infection in their blood for which they need to take medication, and that if they get a cut, they need to clean up the blood themselves.
BCG-associated lymphadenitis has been described in a 9-year-old child from Thailand who received BCG at birth.
Exposure to antiretroviral therapy may then result in selection for previously undetectable mutations associated with drug resistance, the appearance of new mutations, or both.
Since there are no NNRTI mutations, efavirenz is still an active agent and is readily available through the antiretroviral treatment program of the South African government.
